title: South Korea Announces Food Price Cuts Ahead of Holiday Spending Surge
summary: Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs launches discounts on thousands of processed food items. Sixteen major companies slash prices up to 58 percent throughout May. Initiative counters inflation pressures from global commodity volatility.
contents:
Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Rural Affairs (MAFRA) announced discounts on processed foods on Wednesday, April 30, 2026. Sixteen largest food manufacturers participate in the campaign. They reduce prices on 4,373 items through May.

Discounts reach up to 58 percent across retailers and categories. MAFRA enlists industry cooperation to blunt inflation impacts. Heavy family spending characterizes the month.

A ministry official highlighted active government-industry partnership. Officials monitor retail prices to confirm checkout reflections. This effort stabilizes domestic economy amid Middle East volatility.

Policymakers intensify cost-of-living management. Global commodity fluctuations pressure consumer sentiment. The program recalibrates public perceptions on food affordability.

MAFRA continues oversight of market dynamics. Authorities ensure sustained price stabilization. This campaign underscores commitment to economic resilience.
